channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: [Virtual Event] Anatomy of a Data Breach: What to Do if it Happens to You
GitHub Confirms Hack Impacting 3,800 Internal Repositories
item.description: <p>The TeamPCP hacking group accessed the repositories after a GitHub employee installed a poisoned VS Code extension.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/github-confirms-hack-impacting-3800-internal-repositories/">GitHub Confirms Hack Impacting 3,800 Internal Repositories</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Verizon DBIR: Vulnerability Exploits Overtake Credentials as Top Access Vector
item.description: Verizon DBIR finds 31% of data breaches began with software flaws last year

item.title: Shadow MCP Servers: The AI Infrastructure You Can't See | UpGuard
item.description: In 2012, it was Dropbox. In 2026, it’s Shadow MCP. Discover why unvetted AI agents are an invisible threat and how to regain total visibility.

item.title: Interpol's 'Operation Ramz' Pioneers Cross-Region Collabs in Middle East
item.description: While the numbers are modest, the crackdown on cybercrime involved 13 countries in the MENA region, the largest law enforcement collaboration to date.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Grafana GitHub Breach Exposes Source Code via TanStack npm Attack
item.description:
Grafana Labs, on May 19, 2026, said an investigation into its recent breach found no evidence of customer production systems or operations being compromised.
It said the scope of the incident is limited to the Grafana Labs GitHub environment, which includes public and private source code along with internal GitHub repositories.
"After the initial assessment, we found that in addition to source
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: GitHub Investigating TeamPCP Claimed Breach of ~4,000 Internal Repositories
item.description:
GitHub on Tuesday said it's investigating unauthorized access to its internal repositories after the notorious threat actor known as TeamPCP listed the platform's source code and internal organizations for sale on a cybercrime forum.
"While we currently have no evidence of impact to customer information stored outside of GitHub's internal repositories (such as our customers' enterprises,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Verizon DBIR 2026: Vulnerability Exploitation Overtakes Credential Theft as Top Breach Vector
item.description: <p>Verizon’s 2026 DBIR finds vulnerability exploitation has overtaken credential abuse as the leading breach vector, as AI accelerates attacks, patching delays worsen, and ransomware and third-party compromises continue to surge.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/verizon-dbir-2026-vulnerability-exploitation-overtakes-credential-theft-as-top-breach-vector/">Verizon DBIR 2026: Vulnerability Exploitation Overtakes Credential Theft as Top Breach Vector</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: What Will Make AI BOMs Real?
item.description: A brief overview of the forces at play that will get more organizations on board with creating and consuming AI bill of materials (BOMs).
item.dc:creator: Ericka Chickowski, Contributing Writer

item.title: Verizon DBIR: Enterprises Face a Dangerous Vulnerability Glut
item.description: Verizon's &quot;2026 Data Breach Investigations Report&quot; (&quot;DBIR&quot;) finds that exploits are now involved in 31% of initial access for breaches, while patching lags too far behind the bad guys.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Windows Zero-Day Barrage Continues After Patch Tuesday
item.description: YellowKey, GreenPlasma, and MiniPlasma add to the growing list of vulnerabilities a security researcher disclosed over the past six weeks.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: CISA Exposes Secrets, Credentials in 'Private' Repo
item.description: The agency's GitHub repository, publicly available since November 2025, was ironically named &quot;Private-CISA.&quot;
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Stealer Spoofs Google, Microsoft &amp; Apple, Then Backdoors macOS
item.description: The SHub Reaper stealer, which hides behind fake WeChat and Miro installers, marks a shift from ClickFix social engineering to Apple script-based execution.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Trapdoor Android Ad Fraud Scheme Hit 659 Million Daily Bid Requests Using 455 Apps
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new ad fraud and malvertising operation dubbed Trapdoor targeting Android device users.
The activity, per HUMAN's Satori Threat Intelligence and Research Team, encompassed 455 malicious Android apps and 183 threat actor-owned command-and-control (C2) domains, turning the infrastructure into a pipeline for multi-stage fraud.
"Users
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Drupal to Patch Highly Critical Vulnerability at Risk of Quick Exploitation
item.description: <p>Drupal says attackers may develop an exploit for the vulnerability within hours or days.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/drupal-to-patch-highly-critical-vulnerability-at-risk-of-quick-exploitation/">Drupal to Patch Highly Critical Vulnerability at Risk of Quick Exploitation</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Microsoft Disrupts Malware-Signing Service Run by ‘Fox Tempest’
item.description: <p> Fox Tempest provides a service that cybercriminals use to distribute ransomware and other malware disguised as legitimate software.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/microsoft-disrupts-malware-signing-service-run-by-fox-tempest/">Microsoft Disrupts Malware-Signing Service Run by &#8216;Fox Tempest&#8217; </a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Microsoft Takes Down Fox Tempest for Providing Ransomware-Enabling Signing Tool
item.description: Microsoft’s Digital Crimes Unit has taken down the infrastructure of Fox Tempest, a prolific cybercrime-enabling threat group

item.title: DirtyDecrypt PoC Released for Linux Kernel CVE-2026-31635 LPE Vulnerability
item.description: Proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code has now been released for a recently patched security flaw in the Linux kernel that could allow for local privilege escalation (LPE). Dubbed DirtyDecrypt (aka DirtyCBC), the vulnerability was discovered and reported by the Zellic and V12 security team on May 9, 2026, only to be informed by the maintainers that it was a duplicate of a vulnerability that had
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Looking Back, Looking Forward: Digesting a Dynamic Bouillabaisse of Cyber Evolution
item.description: Dark Reading editors reflect on two decades of dramatic change — from perimeter defense to assume-breach strategies — and warn that while AI, cloud, and COVID-19 have transformed the threat landscape, organizations are still failing at fundamental security hygiene that could stop sophisticated attacks in their tracks.
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading Editorial Team

item.title: AdvancedHEALTH Ransomware Claim Includes 2.3M Patient Data Lines
item.description: <p>DragonForce claims it stole 390GB from AdvancedHEALTH, including patient data and minors’ records, as breach notices and legal scrutiny begin.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-advancedhealth-ransomware-patient-data-claim/">AdvancedHEALTH Ransomware Claim Includes 2.3M Patient Data Lines</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: Legacy Windows Tool MSHTA Fuels Surge in Silent Malware Attacks
item.description: <p>Attackers are increasingly abusing Microsoft’s decades-old MSHTA utility to stealthily deliver stealers, loaders, and persistent malware through phishing, fake software downloads, and LOLBIN-based attack chains.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/legacy-windows-tool-mshta-fuels-surge-in-silent-malware-attacks/">Legacy Windows Tool MSHTA Fuels Surge in Silent Malware Attacks</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kevin Townsend

item.title: Unpatched ChromaDB Vulnerability Can Lead to Server Takeover
item.description: <p>The security defect can be exploited remotely, without authentication, to execute arbitrary code and leak sensitive information.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/unpatched-chromadb-vulnerability-can-lead-to-server-takeover/">Unpatched ChromaDB Vulnerability Can Lead to Server Takeover</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: AI Raises the Bar on Vulnerability Awareness and Secure-by-Design Software
item.description: AI-powered vulnerability scanning leaves no excuse for unpatched bugs as the EU Cyber Resilience Act pushes firms toward secure-by-design software

item.title: Agentic AI Accelerates Software Builds and Mobile App Attacks
item.description: Digital.ai data reveals 87% of apps were attacked over the past year

item.title: B1ack’s Stash Marketplace Gives Away 4.6 Million Stolen Credit Cards
item.description: <p>The stolen credit card data was released as a free download, allegedly in response to seller misconduct.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/b1acks-stash-marketplace-gives-away-4-6-million-stolen-credit-cards/">B1ack&#8217;s Stash Marketplace Gives Away 4.6 Million Stolen Credit Cards</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: The New Phishing Click: How OAuth Consent Bypasses MFA
item.description:
In February 2026, a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform called EvilTokens went live. Within five weeks, it had compromised more than 340 Microsoft 365 organizations across five countries.&nbsp;
The targets of the platform received a message asking them to enter a short code at microsoft.com/devicelogin and complete their normal MFA challenge, then walked away believing they had verified a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cyber Resilience is the New Business Continuity Plan
item.description: <p>The organizations best prepared to face disruption are those that align security, continuity and risk management around what the business cannot afford to lose.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/cyber-resilience-is-the-new-business-continuity-plan/">Cyber Resilience is the New Business Continuity Plan</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Steve Durbin

item.title: Laurie Anderson Is Quoting Me
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Drupal to Release Urgent Core Security Updates on May 20, Sites Told to Prepare
item.description:
Drupal has issued an alert stating that it intends to release a "core security release" for all supported branches on May 20, 2026, from 5-9 p.m. UTC.
"The Drupal Security Team urges you to reserve time for core updates at that time because exploits might be developed within hours or days," the maintainers of the PHP-based content management system (CMS) said.
"Not all configurations are
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 201 Arrested in Crackdown on Cybercrime in Middle East, North Africa
item.description: <p>The 13-country effort, named Operation Ramz, targeted cyber threats in the Middle East and North Africa region.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/201-arrested-in-crackdown-on-cybercrime-in-middle-east-north-africa/">201 Arrested in Crackdown on Cybercrime in Middle East, North Africa</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: PoC Released for DirtyDecrypt Linux Kernel Vulnerability
item.description: <p>Patched in April, the underlying vulnerability allows local attackers to elevate their privileges to root.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/poc-released-for-dirtydecrypt-linux-kernel-vulnerability/">PoC Released for DirtyDecrypt Linux Kernel Vulnerability</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: SEPPMail Secure E-Mail Gateway Vulnerabilities Enable RCE and Mail Traffic Access
item.description: Critical security vulnerabilities have been disclosed in SEPPMail Secure E-Mail Gateway, an enterprise-grade email security solution, that could be exploited to achieve remote code execution and enable an attacker to read arbitrary mails from the virtual appliance. "These vulnerabilities could have been exploited to read all mail traffic or as an entry vector into the internal network,"
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Grafana Labs Confirms Hackers Stole Source Code
item.description: Open source tool maker Grafana says hackers stole codebase via GitHub breach

item.title: The quest for greater tech independence
item.description: A complete decoupling from US technology is neither realistic nor necessary, but the changing environment does require nations and companies to reassess their relationships and dependencies

item.title: Hackers Bypass Security Tools to Target Users Directly
item.description: Bridewell report calls out emergence of “fix-style” attacks

item.title: Compromised Nx Console 18.95.0 Targeted VS Code Developers with Credential Stealer
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a compromised version of the Nx Console extension that was published to the Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) Marketplace.
The extension in question is rwl.angular-console (version 18.95.0), a popular user interface and plugin for code editors like VS Code, Cursor, and JetBrains. The VS Code extension has more than 2.2 million installations. The Open
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Popular GitHub Action Tags Redirected to Imposter Commit to Steal CI/CD Credentials
item.description:
In yet another software supply chain attack, threat actors have compromised the popular GitHub Actions workflow, actions-cool/issues-helper, to run malicious code that harvests sensitive credentials and exfiltrates them to an attacker-controlled server.
"Every existing tag in the repository has been moved to point to an imposter commit that does not appear in the action's normal commit history,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Pushes Malicious AntV npm Packages via Compromised Maintainer Account
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a fresh software supply chain attack campaign that has compromised various npm packages associated with the @antv ecosystem as part of the ongoing Mini Shai-Hulud attack wave.
"The attack affects packages tied to the npm maintainer account atool, including echarts-for-react, a widely used React wrapper for Apache ECharts with roughly 1.1 million weekly
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: What is Financial Services Cybersecurity? Threats and Defenses | UpGuard
item.description: Understand the state of modern financial services cybersecurity to protect your assets, ensure compliance, and mitigate systemic risk.

item.title: What is CVSS? A Complete Guide to Vulnerability Scoring | UpGuard
item.description: Master CVSS v4.0. Use EPSS and CISA KEV to prioritize vulnerabilities, reduce alert fatigue, and meet modern regulatory compliance.

item.title: Is 2026 the Year AI Bills of Materials Get Real?
item.description: Understanding AI BOMs and where they fit into risk management for artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Ericka Chickowski, Contributing Writer

item.title: Microsoft Exchange Zero-Day Under Attack, No Patch Available
item.description: CVE-2026-42897 stems from a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability and can allow an attacker to compromise Outlook Web Access (OWA) mailboxes.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: 'Claw Chain' Vulnerabilities Threaten OpenClaw Deployments
item.description: The now-patched vulnerabilities in the rapidly growing AI agent framework allow attackers to steal credentials, escalate privileges, and maintain persistence.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
item.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity &#038; Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: Shai-Hulud Worm Clones Spread After Code Release
item.description: The release of Shai-Hulud source code spells trouble for software developers as researchers worry the self-replicating worm could scale.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: PCI SSC Publishes PCI PTS HSM v5.0
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Grafana Rejects Ransom Demand After GitHub Breach Exposes Codebase Theft
item.description: <p>Grafana refused an extortion demand after attackers used a stolen GitHub token to download code, with no customer data exposed so far.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-grafana-github-token-codebase-breach/">Grafana Rejects Ransom Demand After GitHub Breach Exposes Codebase Theft</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: INTERPOL Operation Ramz Disrupts MENA Cybercrime Networks with 201 Arrests
item.description: INTERPOL has coordinated a first-of-its-kind cybercrime crackdown across the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) that led to 201 arrests and the identification of an additional 382 suspects. The initiative involved the efforts of 13 countries from the region between October 2025 and February 2026, aiming to investigate and neutralize malicious infrastructure, arrest perpetrators behind these
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Fuel Tank Breaches Expand Scope of Iran's Cyber Offensive
item.description: Security experts have long warned that insecure automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems exposed on the Internet can be tampered with by threat actors.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Interpol Launches Sweeping Cybercrime Crackdown in MENA Region
item.description: Over 200 people were arrested in an anti-cybercrime operation that spanned 13 countries across the Middle East and North Africa

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Exchange 0-Day, npm Worm, Fake AI Repo, Cisco Exploit and More
item.description: Monday opens with a trust problem. A mail server flaw is under active use. A network control system was targeted. Trusted packages were poisoned. A fake model page pushed a stealer. Then came the familiar ransom claim: the data was returned and deleted. The pattern is clear. One weak dependency can leak keys. One leaked key can open cloud access. One cloud foothold can become a production
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Infosecurity Europe Cyber Startup Competition: Meet the Finalists
item.description: New for 2026, the Infosecurity Europe Startup competition will see five finalists pitch their ideas in front of a live audience, including senior industry leaders, investors and buyers

item.title: How to Reduce Phishing Exposure Before It Turns into Business Disruption
item.description: What happens when a phishing email looks clean enough to pass through security, but dangerous enough to expose the business after one click? That is the gap many SOCs still struggle with: the attacks that leave teams unsure what was exposed, who else was targeted, and how far the risk has spread. Early phishing detection closes that gap. It helps teams move from uncertainty to evidence faster,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Boring Stuff Is Dangerous Now
item.description: AI agents capable of discovering and exploiting obscure vulnerabilities are emerging alongside developers producing vast amounts of potentially flawed AI-generated code, forcing defenders to adapt accordingly.
item.dc:creator: Shlomie Liberow

item.title: Boulevard of Broken Dreams: 2 Decades of Cyber Fails
item.description: From the MGM and Caesars fiasco and MOVEit's patch nightmare to epic business blunders and the jaded reality of living in a post-breach world, Dark Reading looks back at the mistakes, miscalculations, systemic failures, and cringeworthy moments that still have us shaking our heads.
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading Editorial Team

item.title: Developer Workstations Are Now Part of the Software Supply Chain
item.description: Supply chain attackers are not only trying to slip malicious code into trusted software. They are trying to steal the access that makes trusted software possible. Recently, three separate campaigns hit npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub in a 48-hour window, and all three targeted secrets from developer environments and CI/CD pipelines, including API keys, cloud credentials, SSH keys, and tokens. This is
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Zero-Day Exploit Against Windows BitLocker
item.description: <p>It&#8217;s <a href="https://arstechnica.com/security/2026/05/zero-day-exploit-completely-defeats-default-windows-11-bitlocker-protections/">nasty</a>, but it requires physical access to the computer:</p> <blockquote><p>The exploit, named YellowKey, was <a href="https://github.com/Nightmare-Eclipse/YellowKey">published</a> earlier this week by a researcher who goes by the alias Nightmare-Eclipse. It reliably bypasses default Windows 11 deployments of BitLocker, the full-volume encryption protection Microsoft provides to make disk contents off-limits to anyone without the decryption key, which is stored in a secured piece of hardware known as a trusted platform module (TPM). BitLocker is a mandatory protection for many organizations, including those that contract with governments...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, SAP, VMware, n8n Patch RCE, SQL Injection, Privilege Escalation Flaws
item.description: Ivanti, Fortinet, n8n, SAP, and VMware have released security fixes for various vulnerabilities that could be exploited by bad actors to bypass authentication and execute arbitrary code. Topping the list is a critical flaw impacting Ivanti Xtraction (CVE-2026-8043, CVSS score: 9.6) that could be exploited to achieve information disclosure or client-side attacks. "External control of a file name
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: NCSC Publishes Guidance on Securing Agentic AI Use
item.description: The UK’s National Cyber Security Centre is helping organizations to understand agentic AI security risks

item.title: Security Researchers Find 47 Zero-Days at Pwn2Own Berlin
item.description: The research community was awarded $1.3m as it found dozens of novel vulnerabilities at Pwn2Own Berlin

item.title: Bank of England, FCA and Treasury Raise Alarm Over Frontier AI
item.description: The UK’s financial authorities have set expectations for the sector on cybersecurity and operational resilience

item.title: MiniPlasma Windows 0-Day Enables SYSTEM Privilege Escalation on Fully Patched Systems
item.description: Chaotic Eclipse, the security researcher behind the recently disclosed Windows flaws, YellowKey and GreenPlasma, has released a proof-of-concept (PoC) for a Windows privilege escalation zero-day flaw that grants attackers SYSTEM privileges on fully patched Windows systems. Codenamed MiniPlasma, the vulnerability impacts "cldflt.sys," which refers to the Windows Cloud Files Mini Filter Driver,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Four Malicious npm Packages Deliver Infostealers and Phantom Bot DDoS Malware
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered four new npm packages containing information-stealing malware, one of which is a clone of the Shai-Hulud worm open-sourced by TeamPCP. The list of identified packages is below -
chalk-tempalte (825 Downloads) @deadcode09284814/axios-util (284 Downloads) axois-utils (963 Downloads) color-style-utils (934 Downloads)
"One of the packages (chalk-tempalte)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Pre-Stuxnet Fast16 Malware Tampered with Nuclear Weapons Simulations
item.description: A new analysis of the Lua-based fast16 malware has confirmed that it was a cyber sabotage tool designed to tamper with nuclear weapons testing simulations. According to Broadcom-owned Symantec and Carbon Black teams, the pre-Stuxnet tool was engineered to corrupt uranium-compression simulations that are central to nuclear weapon design. "Fast16's hook engine is selectively interested in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Can Laws Stop Deepfakes? South Korea Aims to Find Out
item.description: South Korea's local elections next month will be a test bed for how effective regulations might be to stymie the flow of deepfakes.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: NGINX CVE-2026-42945 Exploited in the Wild, Causing Worker Crashes and Possible RCE
item.description: A newly disclosed security flaw impacting NGINX Plus and NGINX Open has come under active exploitation in the wild, days after its public disclosure, according to VulnCheck. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-42945 (CVSS score: 9.2), is a heap buffer overflow in ngx_http_rewrite_module affecting NGINX versions 0.6.27 through 1.30.0. According to AI-native security company depthfirst, the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Grafana GitHub Token Breach Led to Codebase Download and Extortion Attempt
item.description:
Grafana has disclosed that an "unauthorized party" obtained a token that granted them the ability to access the company's GitHub environment and download its codebase.
"Our investigation has determined that no customer data or personal information was accessed during this incident, and we have found no evidence of impact to customer systems or operations," Grafana said in a series of
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Funnel Builder Flaw Under Active Exploitation Enables WooCommerce Checkout Skimming
item.description:
A critical security vulnerability impacting the Funnel Builder plugin for WordPress has come under active exploitation in the wild to inject malicious JavaScript code into WooCommerce checkout pages with the goal of stealing payment data.
Details of the activity were published by Sansec this week. The vulnerability currently does not have an official CVE identifier. It
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Bigfin Squid
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Congress Puts Heat on Instructure After Canvas Outage
item.description: The House Committee on Homeland Security sent a letter about the Canvas cyberattack, the same day that the edtech company said it reached an &quot;agreement&quot; with the ShinyHunters cybercriminals.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Vibe Coding Cheat Sheet: Tools, Prompts, Security Tips, and More
item.description: <p>This vibe coding cheat sheet explains how plain-language prompts can build apps fast, plus the planning, testing, and security checks needed.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-vibe-coding-cheat-sheet/">Vibe Coding Cheat Sheet: Tools, Prompts, Security Tips, and More</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: New Windows Update May Undo Bad Driver Updates on Its Own
item.description: <p>Microsoft is testing Cloud-Initiated Driver Recovery, a Windows Update feature designed to roll back bad drivers with less manual IT work.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-windows-driver-recovery-update/">New Windows Update May Undo Bad Driver Updates on Its Own</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Esther Shein

item.title: Turla Turns Kazuar Backdoor Into Modular P2P Botnet for Persistent Access
item.description:
The Russian state-sponsored hacking group known as
Turla
has transformed its custom backdoor Kazuar into a modular peer-to-peer (P2P) botnet that's engineered for stealth and persistent access to compromised hosts.
Turla, per the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), is assessed to be affiliated with Center 16 of Russia's Federal Security Service (FSB)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OpenAI Warns Mac Users to Update Apps After Supply-Chain Attack
item.description: <p>OpenAI says Mac users must update ChatGPT, Codex, and Atlas apps by June 12 after an npm supply-chain attack exposed signing certificates.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-openai-mac-app-update-supply-chain-attack/">OpenAI Warns Mac Users to Update Apps After Supply-Chain Attack</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Two Unpatched Windows Exploits Target BitLocker, SYSTEM Access
item.description: <p>Two unpatched Windows exploit PoCs target BitLocker protections and privilege controls after Microsoft’s May Patch Tuesday security update.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-unpatched-windows-exploits-bitlocker-privilege-escalation/">Two Unpatched Windows Exploits Target BitLocker, SYSTEM Access</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Gremlin Stealer Evolves into Modular Threat with Advanced Evasion Capabilities
item.description: A new Gremlin stealer variant has evolved into a modular toolkit with advanced evasion and data theft capabilities, according to new Unit 42 research

item.title: Request for Comments: PCI Secure Software Lifecycle Standard v2.0
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Four OpenClaw Flaws Enable Data Theft, Privilege Escalation, and Persistence
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a set of four security flaws in OpenClaw that could be chained to achieve data theft, privilege escalation, and persistence.
The vulnerabilities, collectively dubbed
Claw Chain
by Cyera, can permit an attacker to establish a foothold, expose sensitive data, and plant backdoors. A brief description of the flaws is below -
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Reports Severe Zero-Day Flaw in On-Prem Exchange Servers
item.description: The zero-day vulnerability affects on-premises installations for all versions of Exchange Server 2016, 2019 and Subscription Edition

item.title: Cyber Pioneers Ponder Past as Prologue
item.description: Robert &quot;RSnake&quot; Hansen, Katie Moussouris, Rich Mogull, Richard Stiennon, and Bruce Schneier reflect on how their favorite columns penned for Dark Reading over the past 20 years have stood the test of time.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Jackson Higgins, Becky Bracken

item.title: Bypassing On-Camera Age-Verification Checks
item.description: <p>Some AI-based video age-verification checks can be fooled with a <a href="https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/06/some-kids-are-bypassing-age-verification-checks-with-a-fake-mustache/">fake mustache</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: What 45 Days of Watching Your Own Tools Will Tell You About Your Real Attack Surface
item.description: In Your Biggest Security Risk Isn't Malware — It's What You Already Trust, we made a simple argument: the most dangerous activity inside most organizations no longer looks like an attack. It looks like administration. PowerShell, WMIC, netsh, Certutil, MSBuild — the same trusted utilities your IT team uses every day are also the preferred toolkit of modern threat actors. Bitdefender's analysis
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: TanStack Supply Chain Attack Hits Two OpenAI Employee Devices, Forces macOS Updates
item.description: OpenAI has disclosed that two of its employee devices in its corporate environment were impacted via the Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack on TanStack, but noted that no user data, production systems, or intellectual property were compromised or modified in an unauthorized manner. "Upon identification of the malicious activity, we worked quickly to investigate, contain, and take steps to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Why geopolitical turmoil is a gift for scammers, and how to stay safe
item.description: Conflict is a boon for opportunistic fraudsters. Look out for their ploys.

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Deploy New TencShell Malware Against Global Manufacturer
item.description: A suspected China-linked threat actor targeted the Indian branch of a global manufacturer leveraging an open source offensive toolkit

item.title: On-Prem Microsoft Exchange Server CVE-2026-42897 Exploited via Crafted Email
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Six MCP Security Incidents Every Security Leader Should Know | UpGuard
item.description: From registry poisoning to filesystem wipes: discover the 6 MCP security incidents every leader must know to secure their AI agent workflows in 2026.

item.title: CISA Adds Cisco SD-WAN CVE-2026-20182 to KEV After Admin Access Exploits
item.description: The U.S.Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added a newly disclosed vulnerability impacting Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, requiring Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to remediate the issue by May 17, 2026. The vulnerability is a critical authentication bypass tracked as CVE-2026-20182. It's
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Taiwan Bullet Train Hack Highlights Cybersecurity Gaps in Rail Systems
item.description: A Taiwanese student experimenting with software-defined radio technology shut down three bullet trains for nearly an hour, leading to an anti-terrorism response.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: SecurityScorecard Snags Driftnet to Level Up Threat Intelligence
item.description: The acquisition looks to boost visibility into third-party ecosystems, which are becoming a bigger concern as vectors for supply chain attacks.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: Maximum Severity Cisco SD-WAN Bug Exploited in the Wild
item.description: This is the second time this year a threat actor has leveraged a CVSS 10.0 vulnerability in Cisco's network control system.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: The First AI-Crafted Zero-Day Was Easy to Spot. The Next One May Not Be
item.description: <p>Google reported the first confirmed AI-assisted zero-day exploit, raising new concerns about logic flaws, supply chain risk, and containment.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-ai-crafted-zero-day-exploit/">The First AI-Crafted Zero-Day Was Easy to Spot. The Next One May Not Be</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Tim Freestone

item.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Auth Bypass Actively Exploited to Gain Admin Access
item.description: Cisco has released updates to address a maximum-severity authentication bypass flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Controller that it said has been exploited in limited attacks. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20182, carries a CVSS score of 10.0. "A vulnerability in the peering authentication in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller, formerly SD-WAN vSmart, and Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, formerly
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Stealer Backdoor Found in 3 Node-IPC Versions Targeting Developer Secrets
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers are sounding the alarm about what has been described as "malicious activity" in newly published versions of node-ipc. According to Socket and StepSecurity, three different versions of the npm package have been confirmed as malicious -
node-ipc@9.1.6 node-ipc@9.2.3 node-ipc@12.0.1
"Early analysis indicates that node-ipc@9.1.6, node-ipc@9.2.3, and node-ipc@12.0.1
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 'FrostyNeighbor' APT Carefully Targets Govt Orgs in Poland, Ukraine
item.description: Attackers uniquely fingerprint victims before delivering spear-phishing payloads aimed at espionage, in the latest campaign from the Belarussian nation-state threat group.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: PAN-OS RCE, Mythos cURL Bug, AI Tokenizer Attacks, and 10+ Stories
item.description: Everything is still on fire. This week feels dumb in the worst way — bad links, weak checks, fake help desks, shady forum posts, and people turning supply chain attacks into some cursed little game for clout and cash. Half of it feels new. Half of it feels like crap we should have fixed years ago. The mess keeps getting louder: users get tricked, boxes get popped, tools meant for normal work
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Upcoming Speaking Engagements
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Mustang Panda Linked to Updated FDMTP Backdoor in Asia-Pacific Espionage Campaign
item.description: Mustang Panda campaign deploys updated FDMTP backdoor against Asia-Pacific and Japan networks

item.title: Ghostwriter Targets Ukrainian Government With Geofenced PDF Phishing, Cobalt Strike
item.description: The Belarus-aligned threat group known as Ghostwriter has been attributed to a fresh set of attacks targeting governmental organizations in Ukraine. Active since at least 2016, Ghostwriter has been linked to both cyber espionage and influence operations targeting neighboring countries, particularly Ukraine. It's also tracked under the monikers FrostyNeighbor, PUSHCHA, Storm-0257, TA445, UAC‑0057
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Launches Android Spyware Forensics Tool for High-Risk Users
item.description: Google’s Android Advanced Protection Mode is getting a new feature allowing trusted security experts to investigate potential spyware infections
